  {
    "type": "Design",
    "conclusion": "修复 Response.iter_lines 的可重入调用问题，支持多次调用",
    "description": "代码变更通过引入 _iter_lines_state 字典来持久化行缓冲状态，解决了 iter_lines 方法不可重入的问题。但存在一个潜在的设计问题：当 decode_unicode 或 delimiter 参数在多次调用之间发生变化时，会重置 pending 状态，这可能导致数据丢失或不一致。例如，如果第一次调用使用 decode_unicode=False，第二次调用使用 decode_unicode=True，pending 状态会被重置，但原始响应流可能已经部分消耗，导致第二次调用从错误的位置开始解析。",
    "evidence": "if (\n    state[\"decode_unicode\"] != decode_unicode\n    or state[\"delimiter\"] != delimiter\n):\n    state[\"pending\"] = None\n    state[\"decode_unicode\"] = decode_unicode\n    state[\"delimiter\"] = delimiter",
    "advice": "建议在参数变化时抛出异常或警告，明确告知用户不支持在同一个响应对象上使用不同的参数多次调用 iter_lines。或者，更安全的设计是：当参数变化时，重置整个响应流的状态（如果可能），但这可能涉及更复杂的逻辑。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "conclusion": "修复 Response.iter_lines 的可重入调用问题，支持多次调用",
    "description": "在 iter_lines 方法的末尾，存在重复设置 state[\"pending\"] = None 的代码。当 pending 为 None 时，会执行 else 分支再次设置相同的值。这种重复操作虽然无害，但降低了代码的可读性，可能让读者困惑。",
    "evidence": "if pending is not None:\n    state[\"pending\"] = None\n    yield pending\nelse:\n    state[\"pending\"] = None",
    "advice": "建议简化代码，将 state[\"pending\"] = None 移到 yield 语句之后，只需设置一次。例如：\nif pending is not None:\n    yield pending\nstate[\"pending\"] = None",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

commits:
[{'sha': 'ad190688ad692addb5af3cf04baaaec1238356df', 'message': 'Fix Response.iter_lines reentrant calls

Persist line-buffer state across repeated iter_lines() calls on the same
streaming response and add coverage for the reentrant and single-iterator
cases.'}]
review_comments:
['Hi @fr3y123, thanks for the PR. While this is a clever approach, I'm not sure it solves the "re-entrancy" issue. We're now maintaining shared mutable state of the Response which I think violates the concept. Having things like delimiter or pending outside of the method means two readers will inherently corrupt each others data.

There's also the issue of the streaming case which breaks here. That's because we have a single ephemeral source (the raw response). Unless everything can be buffered by iter_content _and_ you enforce the whole stream is read by a single caller on the first pass, you're going to have issues. This is the primary use case for `iter_lines` because otherwise you could simply call `.split()` on `Response.content`.

The initial addition of the note + xfail was likely to indicate a fundamental API limitation rather than documenting something to fix. I don't know how we'd be able to keep track of individual reads in the same manner we can for `iter_content`.', 'Hi @nateprewitt, Thanks for the detailed explanation.

That makes sense. I was reading the xfail as a bug to fix, but your explanation clarified that this is really a limitation of a single streaming source rather than a straightforward correctness bug.

I’m reverted this on my side and closed out the PR. Thanks again for the clear review.']

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Security",
    "conclusion": "修复了NO_PROXY代理绕过漏洞（尾随点FQDN和域名后缀）。",
    "description": "原始代码使用 `parsed.hostname.endswith(host)` 来检查主机名是否匹配 `no_proxy` 列表中的条目。这存在安全漏洞，因为攻击者可以注册一个以 `no_proxy` 条目为后缀的域名来绕过代理设置。例如，如果 `no_proxy='example.com'`，那么 `evilexample.com` 也会被错误地匹配并绕过代理。此外，原始代码没有正确处理主机名末尾的点（FQDN中的尾随点）。新代码通过移除主机名的尾随点、将 `no_proxy` 条目开头的点移除并进行小写化，然后进行精确匹配或后缀匹配（要求后缀以点开头），从而更安全地执行匹配。",
    "evidence": "for host in no_proxy:\n                candidate = host.lstrip(\".\").lower()\n                hostname = parsed.hostname.lower().rstrip(\".\")\n\n                if hostname == candidate or hostname.endswith(\".\" + candidate):\n                    return True\n\n                if parsed.port:\n                    host_with_port = f\"{hostname}:{parsed.port}\"\n                    if host_with_port == candidate or host_with_port.endswith(\".\" + candidate):\n                        return True",
    "advice": "修复逻辑正确。建议添加单元测试，覆盖以下场景：1) 精确域名匹配（如 `example.com` 匹配 `example.com`）；2) 子域名匹配（如 `sub.example.com` 匹配 `.example.com` 或 `example.com`）；3) 带端口号的匹配；4) 尾随点FQDN（如 `example.com.`）；5) 应防止后缀误匹配（如 `evilexample.com` 不应匹配 `example.com`）。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "conclusion": "修复了NO_PROXY代理绕过漏洞（尾随点FQDN和域名后缀）。",
    "description": "新代码中，变量 `host_with_port` 在循环内部被重新定义和赋值。然而，在循环开始之前，已经有一个同名的变量 `host_with_port` 被定义（`host_with_port += f\":{parsed.port}\"`）。这可能会引起混淆，因为循环内部的 `host_with_port` 覆盖了外部的变量，并且两者的用途和格式略有不同（外部变量包含原始主机名，内部变量使用小写化和去尾随点后的主机名）。虽然这不会导致功能错误，但降低了代码的可读性。",
    "evidence": "host_with_port += f\":{parsed.port}\"\n...\n                if parsed.port:\n                    host_with_port = f\"{hostname}:{parsed.port}\"",
    "advice": "建议将循环内部用于端口匹配的变量重命名，例如改为 `hostname_with_port`，以避免与外部变量名冲突，使代码意图更清晰。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

commits:
[{'sha': 'ed724b1e24569d31b5279e6dd5e24401f592ca00', 'message': 'Clarify dependency compatibility warning messages

Replace the vague combined warning "urllib3 (...) or chardet
(...)/charset_normalizer (...) doesn't match a supported version!" with
individual, actionable warnings that identify exactly which dependency
is incompatible and state the supported version range.

This makes it much easier for users to diagnose and resolve the issue,
especially when chardet is pulled in transitively by another package.

Fixes #7284'}]
review_comments: ["Thanks for the PR, @Krishnachaitanyakc. Unfortunately I don't think this is a change we'd take. Chardet gets a major version bump once every 4-5 years historically. The error is intended to show everything in the workspace that may be mismatched, so moving them to individual errors defeats that.\r\n\r\nUsually we ship releases same day as chardet release, or very close, this one has just had some controversy around it. We'll have that addressed in the near future."]

commits:
[{'sha': '71f73e543de5ed2b0221e88f4da64fc16ddd7b6b', 'message': 'Fix HTTPDigestAuth with non-latin (bytes) credentials

When username or password are passed as bytes to HTTPDigestAuth,
the f-string formatting produced b'...' repr in the Digest header
(e.g., username="b'Ond\xc5\99ej'" instead of username="Ondřej").

This fix normalizes bytes to str (via UTF-8 decode) at the start of
build_digest_header, ensuring correct header formatting and digest
computation for both bytes and str credentials.

Fixes #6102'}]

["## 🤖 Sentinel PR Agent — Review Report

**Summary:** The PR correctly fixes proxy precedence by moving the merge_setting calls before the environment proxy lookup, so session-level proxies are not overridden by environment variables. The core logic change is sound. However, there are several issues: a PR_BODY.md file is being committed to the repo, the new test has limited coverage (missing per-request proxy precedence and verify ordering cases), and there is a latent AttributeError risk if proxies resolves to None after merge_setting.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| 🔴 Critical | 0 |
| 🟠 High | 1 |
| 🟡 Medium | 3 |
| 🔵 Low | 3 |

### Issues Found

#### 🟡 [MEDIUM] PR_BODY.md committed to repository
**File:** `PR_BODY.md` (line None)
A PR_BODY.md file describing the pull request has been committed to the repository root. This is a meta-document intended for the GitHub PR description, not for the codebase itself. It will pollute the repository history and confuse future contributors who encounter it in the source tree.
> **Suggestion:** Remove PR_BODY.md from the commit. Use the GitHub PR description field instead, or add PR_BODY.md to .gitignore if it is generated by tooling.

#### 🟡 [MEDIUM] proxies.get() called on potentially None result from merge_setting
**File:** `src/requests/sessions.py` (line 768)
After the reorder, `proxies` is the result of `merge_setting(proxies, self.proxies)`. If both the incoming `proxies` argument and `self.proxies` are `None`, `merge_setting` returns `None` (see merge_setting lines 68-69: if session_setting is None, return request_setting). The subsequent `proxies.get('no_proxy')` on line 768 would then raise an `AttributeError`. While `self.proxies` is initialized to `{}` in `__init__`, this is a latent risk for subclasses, direct callers of `merge_environment_se…
> **Suggestion:** Guard against None after merge_setting: `proxies = merge_setting(proxies, self.proxies) or {}`. Alternatively, ensure the None guard on line 768 is evaluated against the post-merge value, which it already is — but the real fix is to ensure merge_setting never returns None for proxies by defaulting: `proxies = merge_setting(proxies, self.proxies) or {}`.

#### 🔵 [LOW] verify env-variable override behavior subtly changed when verify=None is passed
**File:** `src/requests/sessions.py` (line 762)
Before this change, the execution order was: (1) check env for CA bundle using the raw `verify` argument, (2) then merge with `self.verify`. After the change: (1) merge `verify` with `self.verify` first, (2) then check env. When `verify=None` is passed and `self.verify=True` (the default), the old code checked `if verify is True or verify is None` with `verify=None` (True branch), then `merge_setting(env_ca_path, True)` returned the env CA path. The new code merges first: `merge_setting(None, Tr…
> **Suggestion:** Document this behavioral change explicitly. If the intent is that `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE` should only apply when no CA bundle is configured at all (neither per-request nor session-level), the new behavior is correct and preferable. Add a test case: `session.verify = '/path/to/ca.pem'` with `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE` set, and `verify=None` passed — assert that the session CA path wins.

#### 🟡 [MEDIUM] New test does not cover per-request proxy taking precedence over session proxy
**File:** `tests/test_requests.py` (line 2637)
The new test `test_proxy_env_vars_dont_override_session_proxies` only verifies that `session.proxies` beats environment proxies. It does not test the full precedence chain: per-request proxy > session proxy > env proxy. Specifically, there is no test asserting that a proxy passed directly to `merge_environment_settings(proxies={'http': 'http://per-request-proxy'})` overrides both `session.proxies` and the environment proxy. This is the highest-priority case and should be explicitly covered.
> **Suggestion:** Add a test that passes `proxies={'http': 'http://per-request-proxy.com'}` to `merge_environment_settings` while also setting `session.proxies['http']` and an env var, and asserts the per-request proxy wins. Also add a test that env proxy is used as fallback when neither per-request nor session proxies specify a scheme.

#### 🔵 [LOW] No test for env proxy fallback when session proxy is absent
**File:** `tests/test_requests.py` (line 2637)
The new test only covers the case where `session.proxies` should win over env proxies. There is no regression test confirming that env proxies still apply as a fallback when `session.proxies` does not contain the relevant scheme. This is the 'env proxy as fallback' behavior mentioned in the PR notes, and it should be explicitly tested to prevent future regressions.
> **Suggestion:** Add a test: create a session with no proxies set, set `http_proxy` env var, call `merge_environment_settings`, and assert the env proxy is present in the result.

#### 🔵 [LOW] Test passes proxies={} instead of None, masking a potential None-proxy edge case
**File:** `tests/test_requests.py` (line 2637)
The test calls `merge_environment_settings(proxies={}, ...)`. In the real call path via `Session.request()`, `proxies` is also `proxies or {}` (line 580), so `{}` is realistic. However, external callers of `merge_environment_settings` (it is a public API) may pass `proxies=None`. The behavior when `proxies=None` is passed should be tested, since `merge_setting(None, self.proxies)` returns `self.proxies` directly (the session dict itself, not a copy), and subsequent `setdefault` mutations would m…
> **Suggestion:** Add a test with `proxies=None` to verify correct behavior. Also audit whether `merge_setting` returns a copy or a reference when `request_setting is None` — if it returns `session_setting` directly, mutations via `setdefault` will corrupt `self.proxies`.

#### 🟠 [HIGH] merge_setting returns session dict by reference; setdefault mutates self.proxies in-place
**File:** `src/requests/sessions.py` (line 760)
In `merge_setting`, when `request_setting is None`, the function returns `session_setting` directly (line 72: `return session_setting`). After the reorder, if `proxies=None` is passed to `merge_environment_settings`, `merge_setting(None, self.proxies)` returns `self.proxies` itself (not a copy). The subsequent `proxies.setdefault(k, v)` loop then mutates `self.proxies` directly, permanently adding environment proxy entries to the session's proxy dict. This is a state-corruption bug: after the fi…
> **Suggestion:** Ensure a copy is made before mutation. Either: (1) change the `setdefault` loop to build a new dict: `proxies = {**proxies, **{k: v for k, v in env_proxies.items() if k not in proxies}}`, or (2) explicitly copy after merge_setting: `proxies = dict(merge_setting(proxies, self.proxies) or {})`. Note: this bug existed before this PR when `proxies=None` was passed, but the reorder makes it more likely to be triggered since the merge now happens before the mutation.

commits:
[{'sha': '8997b8edbeb5a3b0b245a5b90d369cd809d0d4ea', 'message': "Fix HTTPDigestAuth bytes representation in Authorization header

When non-Latin credentials are passed as bytes, f-string formatting
produces literal bytes representation (e.g., b'Ond\xc5\x99ej') instead
of proper UTF-8 strings.

This fix decodes bytes credentials to UTF-8 strings before using them
in header construction, ensuring proper character representation.

Fixes #6102"}, {'sha': 'ec72aaf7c78faba639f509dfec6450713c6be8a4', 'message': "Fix HTTPDigestAuth to handle invalid UTF-8 bytes gracefully

When decoding bytes credentials to strings for header formatting,
use errors='replace' to handle invalid UTF-8 sequences. This prevents
UnicodeDecodeError crashes while maintaining RFC 7616 compliance for
valid UTF-8 input.

Fixes #6102"}, {'sha': '3f49a789dd630be7163370945c95eb2d6ae8852b', 'message': "Use strict UTF-8 decoding for HTTPDigestAuth credentials

The previous fix used errors='replace' which silently corrupts invalid
UTF-8 sequences in credentials. This causes authentication failures that
are hard to debug because the corrupted characters are not visible.

Using strict decoding (the default) ensures that invalid UTF-8 input
raises a clear UnicodeDecodeError, alerting users to malformed credentials.

Fixes #6102"}]
